gaps.feature: Skill Authoring editor вҖ” native skill creation (scaffold wizard + 11-typed-block canvas + Guide rail + live deterministic preview + publish/vetting lifecycle + file-upload safety-scan)
gaps.why_uncovered: Distinct from ui E4-S3 'Builder Base+Delta' (which only edits/forks an EXISTING marketplace skill вҖ” AC1 'Given a fork'). authoring.jsx creates a brand-NEW Native skill from a blank/templated scaffold: 2-step wizard (name+intent -> starter template), then a Notion-style block canvas with 11 first-class block kinds (text/instruction/example/placeholder/rule/conditional/tasklist/context/verbatim/file/raw) each with its own typed widget + inline validation, a slash-menu block picker, a re-enterable 'Guide rail' checklist (honest, no score), a live deterministic word-count preview pane, a draft->vetting->published lifecycle with a 'New' badge (never 'Used by 0'), and a file-upload block with a real safety-scan/quarantine flow. Grepped feature-map.txt and every breakdown/*/plan.json for 'native skill', 'scaffold wizard', 'skill authoring', 'block canvas', 'guide rail', 'first-class block', '11 block' вҖ” zero hits. staging E6-S4 'Prompt-pack marketplace' (FR-STG29) and wiki-api's 21-block wiki-authoring are both different domains (wiki content, not Studio skill creation).

verdict.reason: Genuinely unticketed: zero hits in feature-map.txt or any breakdown/*/plan.json for native-skill-creation terms (scaffold wizard, block canvas, Guide rail, 11 block kinds). Confirmed distinct from ui E4-S3, whose AC1 is explicitly scoped to 'Given a fork' (edit/fork an EXISTING skill only) -- no scaffold, no block-kind picker, no publish lifecycle for a brand-new Native skill. POC source web/src/prototype/screens/authoring.jsx confirmed to exist at exactly 918 lines as claimed. Genuine product functionality (a first-class authoring surface), not POC scaffolding or a duplicate of another gap in this batch.

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Skill Authoring editor вҖ” native scaffold wizard + 11-block canvas + Guide rail + publish lifecycle
proposed_issue.body_md:
## рҹҺҜ Story

As a **user**, I want **to create a brand-new skill from a 2-step scaffold (name+intent -> starter template) that dissolves into a Notion-style block canvas of 11 typed block kinds with a slash-menu, a re-enterable Guide rail, a live deterministic preview, and a draft->vetting->published lifecycle** so that **I can author a skill from scratch without ever touching a blank prompt box or seeing a fake capture score** (extends FR-UI12/FR-UI13 authoring surface family).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestAuthoringScaffoldToPublishedNativeSkill` вҖ” an integration test asserting S1 (name+intent) -> S2 (starter template pick) mints a Native draft with >=1 non-empty section, the canvas renders all 11 block kinds via the slash-menu with per-kind inline validation, the Guide rail's publish-readiness gate matches `hasName && blockCount>0`, the live preview renders byte-stable deterministic text (no LLM) that matches Copy's output exactly, and Publish transitions draft->vetting->published with a 'New' badge (never 'Used by 0'), verified through the render + state machine. *Final H1вҖ“H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## вң… Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** вҖ” Given S1, When a name is entered (intent optional), Then 'Next' unlocks; When S2 is reached, Then the 11 block kinds (Text/Instruction/Example/Placeholder/Rule/Conditional/Tasklist/Context/Verbatim/File/Raw) are shown with plain-language descriptions and 2+ advanced kinds are labelled 'Advanced'. *Assertion: 11 kinds rendered; advanced kinds tagged.* [Source: POC web/src/prototype/screens/authoring.jsx L20-52]
- [ ] **AC2** вҖ” Given the canvas, When 'Add a block' or `/` is used, Then a filterable slash-menu lists all kinds and mints a typed block via a per-kind default-content factory; each kind renders its own editing widget (e.g. Example = input+output+few-shot checkbox; Placeholder = name+type+options+default+required; Rule = must/must-not polarity). *Assertion: slash-menu filters by label; each kind's widget matches its content shape.* [Source: POC authoring.jsx L94-96, L607-655, L724-863]
- [ ] **AC3** вҖ” Given a block, When content is incomplete, Then an inline plain-English validation hint renders (e.g. Example needs both input+output; Placeholder needs a name; Conditional needs cond+then) вҖ” never a blocking error. *Assertion: hint text matches the per-kind rule; block remains editable.* [Source: POC authoring.jsx L707-721]
- [ ] **AC4** вҖ” Given the Guide rail, When toggled open, Then it shows a live, honest, no-score checklist (name set / intent set / N blocks in M/M' sections / publish-ready) and can add/edit intent without leaving the canvas (re-enterable wizard, no second editor). *Assertion: checklist booleans derive from real state; intent edits from the rail persist to the same Delta.* [Source: POC authoring.jsx L152-164, L461-497]
- [ ] **AC5** вҖ” Given the live preview dock, When any block changes, Then the preview re-renders deterministically (pure function, no LLM, same input -> same bytes) with an approximate (labelled, non-token) word count, and Copy emits the exact same plain text. *Assertion: preview text === Copy payload; disabled/Raw/Verbatim blocks pass through byte-for-byte, never reflowed.* [Source: POC authoring.jsx L104-146, L563-587]
- [ ] **AC6 (lifecycle)** вҖ” Given a publish-ready draft, When Publish is pressed, Then status moves draft -> vetting -> published, mints v1, and shows a 'New' badge вҖ” never a fabricated 'Used by 0'; editing a published skill returns it to draft-until-republish (honest, non-silent). *Assertion: status transitions in order; badge text never implies false usage.* [Source: POC authoring.jsx L179-181, L253-258, L387-414]
- [ ] **AC7 (file block, negative)** вҖ” Given a File block, When an unsupported file type is uploaded, Then it is held (quarantined) with an honest 'not added and not lost' message and a supported-type nudge вҖ” never silently dropped. *Assertion: flagged state renders the quarantine copy; no silent loss.* [Source: POC authoring.jsx L866-916]

## рҹ”Ё Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestAuthoringScaffoldToPublishedNativeSkill` (AC1/AC5/AC6).
- [ ] GREEN: S1/S2 scaffold wizard + starter templates (AC1); block factory + slash-menu + 11 per-kind widgets (AC2); inline validation (AC3); Guide rail checklist + re-enterable intent (AC4); deterministic weave/preview + byte-faithful Copy (AC5); publish lifecycle + New badge (AC6); file-upload safety-scan + quarantine (AC7).

## рҹ§  Context

**рҹ§ҫ Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep. E4-S3 'Builder Base+Delta' only covers editing/forking an EXISTING skill ('Given a fork' вҖ” AC1); it has no scaffold wizard, no 11-block-kind picker, no Guide rail, and no draft->vetting->published lifecycle for a brand-new Native skill. Grepped feature-map.txt (740 lines, 615+ stories) and every breakdown/*/plan.json for native-skill-creation terms вҖ” zero hits; the only 'publish' hits outside infra were staging's wiki-content prompt-pack marketplace (FR-STG29, different domain) and the api-side reputation ledger (E2-S3, backend only вҖ” no UI renders it). This story is the missing front-end authoring surface; it shares the deterministic-weave substrate with E4-S3 and should reuse rather than reimplement it.

React-front authoring editor (CS В§6 вҖ” the deterministic render is presentational, no-LLM, matching the Builder's weave contract). Seam: BFF skill-create/Delta persist (reuse E4-S3's persistence seam). Sibling dependency: E4-S3 (shares the weave/Delta model), E1-S5 (honest states), E1-S3 (DS tokens for the 11 block-kind badges).

## рҹ§Ә Testing

`TestAuthoringScaffoldToPublishedNativeSkill` (integration) + unit tests per block-kind widget + the deterministic-weave pure function. CS В§5 mocking: BFF skill-create fixtures; never mock own weave/preview.

## рҹ“Ҹ Guidance

- CS `6aMAzsYeQb`: В§6 shallow view В· В§11 honest (no fake score, no silent file loss) В· В§3 naming (block-kind labels) В· В§5 fixture the sibling (E4-S3 weave).
- SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: honesty lens (lossless Raw catch-all, 'New' never 'Used by 0'); no-data-loss lens (disable-not-delete, quarantine not drop).
- Cell-lint `JGAUQRsw2g`: N/A runtime.

## рҹ”— References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (FR-UI12/FR-UI13 family) В· POC `web/src/prototype/screens/authoring.jsx` (ground truth, 918 lines) В· skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md В§3.1/В§4 (referenced in POC header comment).

## рҹ”— Dependencies

- [ ] **Blocked by:** ENG-2109 (Definition Pack вҖ” the contract TAG is the dispatch gate).
- [ ] **Parent epic:** E4 (ui: Discover, Skill Viewer & Builder) вҖ” sibling to E4-S3.
- [ ] **Intra-service order:** after E4-S2 (Skill Viewer), shares substrate with E4-S3 (Builder weave).

## рҹ“Ў Protocol

CLAIM вҶ’ PLAN вҶ’ PROGRESS вҶ’ COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) вҶ’ HANDOFF вҶ’ REVIEW (reviewer вү  implementer) вҶ’ TICK вҶ’ DONE (orchestrator-only) вҶ’ ESCALATE.

gaps.feature: Skill Review Inbox вҖ” owner feedback queue: comments + suggestions across owned skills, per-block diff accept/reject (partial-accept), decomposable reputation chips, rebase-conflict handling, comment-to-suggestion conversion
gaps.why_uncovered: The api-side backend for this exists (E2-S3 'Marketplace, social & append-only reputation ledger', FR-SA3: comments/suggestions/use-gated reviews/reputation_ledger) but NO ui story renders it. The two closest ui hits are both different domains: E6-S4 'Curator desk + Curation Queue' is explicitly WIKI-content proposals from the Librarian (AC1: 'distinct from Your Wiki... MCP capture, imports') and staging E4-S3 'Review Queue' (FR-STG12) is a wiki ChangeSet queue. Neither renders skill-owner suggestion/comment triage. Grepped feature-map.txt + all breakdown bodies for 'suggestion', 'reputation', 'comment thread', 'rebase' in a marketplace/skill context вҖ” only the backend ledger story surfaced, never a consuming UI story.

verdict.reason: Genuinely unticketed: the api-side backend (E2-S3, reputation_ledger/comments/suggestions, FR-SA3) exists but grepping ui/plan.json and extension/plan.json plus feature-map.txt for suggestion/reputation/rebase/comment-thread/review-inbox surfaces no consuming UI story. Boundary claims verified against source: E6-S4 AC1 is explicitly 'distinct from Your Wiki' and scoped to Librarian wiki-content proposals (MCP capture, imports) -- a different domain from marketplace skill-owner suggestion/comment triage. POC source web/src/prototype/screens/review.jsx exists (784 lines, POC claims 785 -- immaterial rounding). Milestone 'NEW' matches the established convention already used for this same J5 screen in the sibling poc-sweep/screens.json output.

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Skill Review Inbox вҖ” owner queue for suggestions + comments with per-block diff decide
proposed_issue.body_md:
## рҹҺҜ Story

As a **skill owner**, I want **one inbox for every comment and change-suggestion on the skills I own, with per-block diff accept/reject (partial-accept mints a new version), a decomposable (never opaque-score) reputation chip per contributor, rebase-conflict surfacing, and comment-to-suggestion conversion** so that **I can triage community feedback efficiently without ever landing a change I didn't explicitly approve** (consumes api E2-S3's `reputation_ledger`/comments/suggestions surface, FR-SA3).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestReviewInboxPerBlockDecideNeverAutoLands` вҖ” an integration test asserting the inbox lists comments+suggestions scoped to skills the signed-in user owns (never another user's), a suggestion's per-block diff can be accepted/rejected independently (partial-accept mints a new version crediting the contributor; reject records no reputation penalty), a rebase-conflict suggestion shows the re-anchored diff with an explicit rebase banner before any decision is possible, and nothing merges into the skill without an explicit per-block or per-group Accept, verified through the render + decision state. *Final H1вҖ“H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## вң… Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** вҖ” Given the inbox, When opened, Then it lists comments+suggestions scoped ONLY to skills owned by the signed-in user, with per-skill scope rows, type/status filters (suggestion/comment, pending/open/needs-rebase), and honest counts ('вҖ”' not '0'). *Assertion: scope excludes non-owned skills; empty counts render 'вҖ”'.* [Source: POC web/src/prototype/screens/review.jsx L117-119, L570-575, L594-627]
- [ ] **AC2** вҖ” Given a suggestion, When opened, Then each touched block renders as an independent before/after diff (non-colour-alone +/вҲ’ markers) with its own Accept/Reject, plus an 'Accept all'/'Reject all' shortcut; accepting a subset publishes a new version crediting the contributor and shows a live post-merge preview (Original <-> With-this-change) with an approximate word-count delta. *Assertion: per-block decision state is independent; accepted-subset preview reflects only accepted blocks.* [Source: POC review.jsx L214-269, L271-427]
- [ ] **AC3** вҖ” Given a contributor, When their reputation chip is expanded, Then it shows a decomposable, itemized track record (accepted count, distinct skills, identity verification, recency, an explicit 'never a black-box score' note) sourced from the append-only `reputation_ledger` вҖ” never a single opaque number. *Assertion: expanded panel lists >=4 itemized facts, no composite score rendered.* [Source: POC review.jsx L132-180]
- [ ] **AC4 (conflict)** вҖ” Given a suggestion whose base block advanced since it was authored, When shown, Then a 'needs-rebase' status pill + banner explain the conflict and the diff is re-anchored against the current block before any decision is enabled. *Assertion: needs-rebase suggestions show the rebase banner; diff matches the current base, not the stale one.* [Source: POC review.jsx L68-84, L328-338]
- [ ] **AC5** вҖ” Given a comment thread, When viewed, Then the anchored block renders read-only (comments never edit), replies are chronological, and 'Turn into a suggestion' opens a pre-anchored suggestion linked back to the thread; 'Resolve with a decision' closes the thread and notifies the contributor. *Assertion: anchor block is read-only; turn-into-suggestion pre-anchors to the same block.* [Source: POC review.jsx L429-499]
- [ ] **AC6 (negative)** вҖ” Given Memory, When the inbox renders, Then no Memory item appears and no reputation chip mounts on any Memory surface (RC-1 вҖ” Memory stays private, decisions happen only in this inbox). *Assertion: inbox never reads/renders Memory; reputation chip absent from Memory routes.* [Source: POC review.jsx L10-17, L606-607]

## рҹ”Ё Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestReviewInboxPerBlockDecideNeverAutoLands` (AC2/AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: owner-scoped inbox + filters/sort (AC1); per-block diff decide + partial-accept + post-merge preview (AC2); decomposable reputation chip (AC3); rebase-conflict banner + re-anchored diff (AC4); comment thread + turn-into-suggestion + resolve (AC5); Memory-exclusion guard (AC6).

## рҹ§  Context

**рҹ§ҫ Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep. api E2-S3 builds the `reputation_ledger`/comments/suggestions/use-gated-reviews backend (FR-SA3) but no consuming UI story exists. Confirmed distinct from E6-S4 Curator desk (explicitly wiki-content proposals from the Librarian, PO-boundary-separated from 'Your Wiki') and from staging's Review Queue (FR-STG12, wiki ChangeSets) вҖ” neither renders skill-marketplace suggestion/comment triage. Grepped feature-map.txt (740 lines) and every breakdown/*/plan.json/bodies/*.md for 'suggestion', 'reputation', 'rebase', 'comment thread' in this context вҖ” the only hits were the backend ledger story and two unrelated wiki-content queues.

React-front review surface (CS В§6). Seam: BFF marketplace comments/suggestions/reputation read+decide (api E2-S3's surface). Sibling dependency: E4-S2 (Skill Viewer, shares block/section labels), E4-S3 (Builder, shares the version-mint-on-accept path), E1-S3 (draft-vs-committed proposal-rendering layer вҖ” likely reusable for the diff UI).

## рҹ§Ә Testing

`TestReviewInboxPerBlockDecideNeverAutoLands` (integration) + unit tests on per-block decision reducer + reputation-tier ranking. CS В§5 mocking: BFF marketplace/reputation fixtures; never mock own decision state.

## рҹ“Ҹ Guidance

- CS `6aMAzsYeQb`: В§6 shallow view В· В§11 honest (decomposable reputation, no opaque score, honest 'вҖ”' counts) В· В§5 fixture the sibling.
- SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: opaque-score anti-pattern lens; no-silent-merge lens (nothing lands without recorded decision, mirrors E6-S4's AC4 pattern); privacy lens (Memory never appears here, RC-1).
- Cell-lint `JGAUQRsw2g`: N/A runtime.

## рҹ”— References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (FR-UI family, TBD exact FR at pack certification) В· api PRD `85qj2wwU2L` (FR-SA3, `reputation_ledger`) В· POC `web/src/prototype/screens/review.jsx` (ground truth, 785 lines) В· skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md В§3.3 (A1 inbox / A2 diff+decision / A3 comment thread, referenced in POC header comment).

## рҹ”— Dependencies

- [ ] **Blocked by:** ENG-2109 (Definition Pack вҖ” the contract TAG is the dispatch gate); api E2-S3 (reputation_ledger/comments/suggestions backend, ENG-2098-gated).
- [ ] **Parent epic:** NEW (or fold into E4 'Discover, Skill Viewer & Builder' at pack certification вҖ” PO to resolve placement).
- [ ] **Intra-service order:** after E4-S2/E4-S3 (shares block/version model), after api E2-S3 ships.

## рҹ“Ў Protocol

CLAIM вҶ’ PLAN вҶ’ PROGRESS вҶ’ COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) вҶ’ HANDOFF вҶ’ REVIEW (reviewer вү  implementer) вҶ’ TICK вҶ’ DONE (orchestrator-only) вҶ’ ESCALATE.

gaps.feature: "Your AIs" cross-AI delivery preferences panel вҖ” default-AI picker, per-provider (ChatGPT/Claude) show/hide toggle, ChatGPT temporary-chat + web-search-on toggles, honest preferred-model picker
gaps.why_uncovered: Distinct from extension E2-S3 'Per-assistant connection-status surface' (that is the BROWSER EXTENSION's MCP-agent connection status) and from ui E7-S2 'Connections & MCP setup' (that is token issuance for AI AGENTS/MCP clients, FR-UI25 вҖ” a different concept). This panel governs the end-user's PREFERENCES for the deep-link 'Open it in your AI' flow consumed by E5-S3 Five-AI export: which providers show, which is default, and two real per-provider link-params (ChatGPT temporary-chat / web-search) plus an honestly-labelled (not-yet-enforced) preferred-model picker. No ui story owns a settings SURFACE for these preferences вҖ” E5-S3 only covers the export/open action itself, not the settings that configure it.

verdict.reason: Genuinely unticketed: zero hits anywhere for 'your ai'/'default ai'/'preferred model'/'temporary-chat'. Boundary claims verified: ui E5-S3 owns only the export ACTION (five formatters + clipboard fallback), no AC for a settings surface; ui E7-S2 is MCP/agent token issuance (FR-UI25), a different concept from end-user chat-provider export preferences; extension E2-S3 is the browser-extension popup's per-assistant MCP connection status, also a different surface. POC source web/src/prototype/screens/account.jsx (872 lines) contains YourAisPanel at the claimed region (L302-485) with the claimed ChatGPT temporary-chat/web-search params and default-provider fallback logic confirmed by direct read.

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Account: Your AIs вҖ” cross-AI delivery preferences (default AI, per-provider toggles, model pref)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## рҹҺҜ Story

As a **user**, I want **an Account settings panel that lets me pick which AI providers show on the 'Open it in your AI' export step, set a default among the enabled ones, toggle ChatGPT's temporary-chat and web-search-on link params, and record a preferred model per provider (honestly labelled as a preference, not yet enforced)** so that **the Five-AI export flow opens in the AI I actually want, the way I want** (consumed by E5-S3 Five-AI export; extends FR-UI16's provider set with a settings surface).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestYourAisPrefsGovernExportDefaultOrdering` вҖ” an integration test asserting: only providers toggled 'on' appear on the Five-AI export step, the chosen default among enabled providers leads that list, disabling the current default falls back to the next enabled provider (never a dead default), ChatGPT's temporary-chat/web-search toggles are dimmed+disabled when ChatGPT is off, and the preferred-model picker persists a preference without falsely claiming the link enforces it, verified through the render + persisted-prefs state consumed by the export step. *Final H1вҖ“H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## вң… Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** вҖ” Given the panel, When a provider's 'Show in Open it in your AI' toggle is switched, Then that provider appears/disappears from the Five-AI export step (E5-S3) on next render; if no provider is enabled, an honest 'no AI selected' state renders on the export step (never a broken/empty button row). *Assertion: export step's rendered provider list matches the toggle state.* [Source: POC web/src/prototype/screens/account.jsx L309-323, POC screens/useit.jsx L224-231]
- [ ] **AC2** вҖ” Given >=1 enabled provider, When 'Default AI' is set via the segmented control, Then only enabled providers are selectable, and if the stored default is later disabled, the effective default falls back to the next enabled provider automatically (no dead/hidden default). *Assertion: default selector options == enabled set; disabling the default reassigns it.* [Source: POC account.jsx L313-326, L367-395]
- [ ] **AC3** вҖ” Given ChatGPT is enabled, When 'Temporary chat' or 'Start with web search on' is toggled, Then the saved preference changes the export deep-link's query params (`&temporary-chat=true`, `&hints=search`); when ChatGPT is OFF, both toggles render visually dimmed and non-interactive. *Assertion: link param presence matches toggle state; toggles inert when provider off.* [Source: POC account.jsx L420-449]
- [ ] **AC4 (honesty)** вҖ” Given a preferred-model picker (per provider), When a non-'auto' model is chosen, Then the preference persists and the UI states plainly that the current deep-link cannot enforce it (opens the AI's current model) вҖ” no false claim of enforcement. *Assertion: preference persists; disclosure copy renders whenever a non-auto model is set.* [Source: POC account.jsx L328-361]
- [ ] **AC5** вҖ” Given any toggle/picker change, When saved, Then a per-field 'Saved' confirmation flashes (aria-live) and the change is durable across reload. *Assertion: aria-live Saved indicator fires; reload reflects the persisted value.* [Source: POC account.jsx L94-102, L311-312]

## рҹ”Ё Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestYourAisPrefsGovernExportDefaultOrdering` (AC1/AC2).
- [ ] GREEN: per-provider show/hide toggle (AC1); default-AI selector with enabled-only options + fallback (AC2); ChatGPT param toggles wired to the export deep-link (AC3); honest preferred-model picker (AC4); per-field Saved confirmation + persistence (AC5).

## рҹ§  Context

**рҹ§ҫ Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep. Confirmed distinct from extension E2-S3 (browser-extension MCP-agent connection status) and ui E7-S2 'Connections & MCP setup' (FR-UI25, token issuance for AI AGENTS вҖ” a different concept from end-user chat-provider export preferences). E5-S3 'Five-AI export' owns the EXPORT ACTION but has no AC for a settings surface that configures which providers show/default or the per-provider link params. Grepped feature-map.txt + all breakdown/*/plan.json for 'Your AIs', 'default AI', 'preferred model', 'temporary-chat' вҖ” zero hits.

React-front settings surface (CS В§6). Seam: BFF preference read/write (small key-value; no Clerk/identity mint involved вҖ” this is app-level UI preference, not auth). Sibling dependency: E5-S3 (consumes these prefs at export time), E1-S6 (entitlement вҖ” N/A here, prefs are Free-tier).

## рҹ§Ә Testing

`TestYourAisPrefsGovernExportDefaultOrdering` (integration, spans this panel + E5-S3's render) + unit tests on the default-fallback logic. CS В§5 mocking: BFF prefs-store fixtures; never mock own toggle/picker state.

## рҹ“Ҹ Guidance

- CS `6aMAzsYeQb`: В§6 shallow view В· В§11 honest (no false enforcement claim on model pref; no dead default) В· В§3 naming.
- SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: honesty lens (disclosed, not hidden, limitation on model enforcement).
- Cell-lint `JGAUQRsw2g`: N/A runtime.

## рҹ”— References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (FR-UI16 family) В· POC `web/src/prototype/screens/account.jsx` L302-485 (ground truth) В· secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md В§3 (referenced in POC header comment).

## рҹ”— Dependencies

- [ ] **Blocked by:** ENG-2109 (Definition Pack вҖ” the contract TAG is the dispatch gate).
- [ ] **Parent epic:** E7 (ui: Your Wiki, Connections & Billing) вҖ” sibling to E7-S2.
- [ ] **Intra-service order:** before/parallel to E5-S3 (Five-AI export consumes these prefs).

## рҹ“Ў Protocol

CLAIM вҶ’ PLAN вҶ’ PROGRESS вҶ’ COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) вҶ’ HANDOFF вҶ’ REVIEW (reviewer вү  implementer) вҶ’ TICK вҶ’ DONE (orchestrator-only) вҶ’ ESCALATE.

gaps.feature: Account Notifications settings вҖ” in-app/email preference matrix grouped by event, with honest 'coming' pills for any not-yet-wired toggle
gaps.why_uncovered: No ui, api, or knowledge story owns a user-facing notification-preferences surface. Grepped feature-map.txt and every breakdown/*/plan.json for 'notification' вҖ” the only hits (ai's 'Per-tenant model config' and knowledge's 'events admin surfaces') are unrelated backend/admin concerns, not an end-user preference matrix. This is a distinct, honesty-pattern-heavy surface (never a fake switch) called out in the PO's account/settings spec that has no owning story.

verdict.reason: Genuinely unticketed: grepped feature-map.txt and every breakdown/*/plan.json for 'notification' -- zero UI-facing hits (only unrelated ai/knowledge backend-admin concerns, confirmed by direct grep). Genuine, distinct product functionality (user notification-preference matrix) not covered by any existing story and not a duplicate of the other four account-panel gaps in this batch. proposed_issue.title: [ui] Account: Notifications вҖ” in-app/email preference matrix with honest 'coming' states
proposed_issue.body_md:
## рҹҺҜ Story

As a **user**, I want **an Account -> Notifications settings panel that shows every notification event grouped (feedback on your skills / your skills' health / digest) with independent in-app and email toggles, saving automatically, and rendering an honest 'coming' pill instead of a fake switch on any channel not yet wired** so that **I control what interrupts me and am never shown a toggle that silently does nothing** (Account settings family, FR-UI to be pinned at pack certification).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestNotificationMatrixNeverFakesAnUnwiredToggle` вҖ” a component-integration test asserting the matrix renders grouped rows from a canon-derived event list, each cell independently renders either a live toggle (persists on change, aria-live Saved) or a 'coming' pill (never a switch bound to nothing) based on a `wired` flag per row/channel, and a channel that is structurally N/A for a row (e.g. no in-app for a digest-only row) renders 'вҖ”' rather than a disabled control, verified through the rendered matrix + persisted-toggle state. *Final H1вҖ“H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## вң… Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** вҖ” Given the panel, When rendered, Then events are grouped (e.g. 'Feedback on your skills', 'Your skills' health', 'Digest') with each row showing its label and, where relevant, a frequency hint (e.g. 'Weekly, batched'). *Assertion: >=3 groups render; hint text present where declared.* [Source: POC web/src/prototype/screens/account.jsx L67-88, L655-671]
- [ ] **AC2** вҖ” Given a row+channel combination, When its `wired` flag is true, Then a real toggle renders that persists on change with a per-cell aria-live 'Saved' confirmation. *Assertion: toggling a wired cell persists and flashes Saved.* [Source: POC account.jsx L610-639]
- [ ] **AC3 (honesty)** вҖ” Given a row+channel combination, When its `wired` flag is false, Then a 'coming' pill renders instead of any switch вҖ” never an interactive control bound to nothing. *Assertion: unwired cells render the Coming affordance, not a toggle.* [Source: POC account.jsx L133-142, L628-631]
- [ ] **AC4 (negative)** вҖ” Given a row where a channel is structurally not applicable (e.g. digest has no in-app channel), When rendered, Then the cell shows 'вҖ”', distinct from both a live toggle and a 'coming' pill. *Assertion: N/A cells render 'вҖ”' only.* [Source: POC account.jsx L84-88, L629-630]
- [ ] **AC5** вҖ” Given the panel, When it mounts, Then it is reachable only via Account -> Notifications (not a 7th sidebar destination) and changes save automatically with no explicit Save button. *Assertion: no dedicated Save action exists; every change persists on toggle.* [Source: POC account.jsx L9-11, L642]

## рҹ”Ё Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestNotificationMatrixNeverFakesAnUnwiredToggle` (AC2/AC3).
- [ ] GREEN: grouped matrix render (AC1); wired live toggle + autosave + Saved flash (AC2); honest Coming pill for unwired cells (AC3); N/A 'вҖ”' cells (AC4); Account-section wiring, no dedicated Save (AC5).

## рҹ§  Context

**рҹ§ҫ Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep. Grepped feature-map.txt (740 lines, 615+ stories) and every breakdown/*/plan.json for 'notification' вҖ” the only two hits (ai's per-tenant model-config settings API, knowledge's events-admin surfaces) are unrelated backend/admin concerns, not an end-user notification-preference matrix. No ui story references this panel at all.

React-front settings surface (CS В§6). Seam: BFF notification-prefs read/write; the `wired` flag itself should be BFF-declared (not hardcoded client-side) so shipping a new channel is a data change, not a redeploy. Sibling dependency: E2-S1 (account entry point via sidebar footer / вҢҳK, not a 7th destination), E1-S5 (honest-states primitives вҖ” reuse for the Coming/'вҖ”' pattern).

## рҹ§Ә Testing

`TestNotificationMatrixNeverFakesAnUnwiredToggle` (component-integration) + unit tests on the wired/unwired/N-A cell-resolution logic. CS В§5 mocking: BFF notification-prefs fixtures; never mock own matrix render.

## рҹ“Ҹ Guidance

- CS `6aMAzsYeQb`: В§6 shallow view В· В§11 honest (no fake switch вҖ” this is the canonical POC example of the pattern) В· В§3 naming.
- SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: honesty lens (dead-control anti-pattern is explicitly what this story exists to prevent).
- Cell-lint `JGAUQRsw2g`: N/A runtime.

## рҹ”— References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (Account/settings family, FR-UI TBD at pack certification) В· POC `web/src/prototype/screens/account.jsx` L65-89, L609-677 (ground truth) В· secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md В§3.5 (referenced in POC header comment).

## рҹ”— Dependencies

- [ ] **Blocked by:** ENG-2109 (Definition Pack вҖ” the contract TAG is the dispatch gate).
- [ ] **Parent epic:** E7 (ui: Your Wiki, Connections & Billing) вҖ” Account-settings family.
- [ ] **Intra-service order:** after E2-S1 (account entry point exists).

## рҹ“Ў Protocol

CLAIM вҶ’ PLAN вҶ’ PROGRESS вҶ’ COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) вҶ’ HANDOFF вҶ’ REVIEW (reviewer вү  implementer) вҶ’ TICK вҶ’ DONE (orchestrator-only) вҶ’ ESCALATE.

gaps.feature: Account Danger Zone вҖ” sign-out, re-auth-gated typed-confirmation account deletion (published skills unclaimed not deleted), quick link to Memory-only hard-delete
gaps.why_uncovered: E3-S4 covers hard-delete of Memory ONLY (FR-UI8, 'prominent action on Memory or Account surfaces'); it has no AC for whole-ACCOUNT deletion, sign-out, the re-authentication gate, the typed-confirmation (email-or-DELETE) pattern, or the specific business rule that published skills are unclaimed-not-deleted on account deletion so other users' usage isn't broken. Grepped feature-map.txt and every breakdown/*/plan.json for 'danger zone', 'delete account', 'sign out' вҖ” zero hits anywhere in the program.

verdict.reason: Genuinely unticketed: zero hits anywhere for 'danger zone'/'delete account'/'sign out'. Boundary claim verified: ui E3-S4 AC4 only requires hard-delete to be 'the prominent action' on Memory or Account surfaces (Memory-scoped consent/hard-delete story) -- it has no AC for whole-account deletion, sign-out, a re-auth gate, or typed-confirmation, and the proposed story correctly reuses (deep-links to, does not duplicate) E3-S4's Memory hard-delete. POC source account.jsx L737-818 (DangerPanel, re-auth stage + typed-confirm match against email-or-DELETE) confirmed present by direct read.

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Account: Danger zone вҖ” sign-out + re-auth-gated typed-confirmation account deletion
proposed_issue.body_md:
## рҹҺҜ Story

As a **user**, I want **an Account -> Danger zone with a plain sign-out action and a separate, re-authentication-gated, typed-confirmation account-deletion flow that plainly states published skills are unclaimed (not deleted) so other users aren't broken** so that **I can leave the platform cleanly and irreversibly only when I clearly mean to** (extends FR-UI8's hard-delete-is-prominent pattern to the whole-account case).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestAccountDeleteRequiresReauthAndTypedConfirm` вҖ” an integration test asserting: Sign out is a single-step, non-destructive action reachable independently of deletion; 'Delete account' cannot proceed to the confirm step without first passing a re-authentication stage; the final confirm step requires typing the account's own email OR the literal string DELETE before the destructive button enables; and on confirmed deletion the copy states published skills are unclaimed (attribution kept) rather than deleted, verified through the render + stage-gated state machine. *Final H1вҖ“H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## вң… Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** вҖ” Given the Danger zone, When Sign out is pressed, Then the session ends immediately with no confirmation gate (low-risk, reversible action) and the user lands on the public marketing route. *Assertion: sign-out is one click; no typed-confirm required.* [Source: POC web/src/prototype/screens/account.jsx L746-755]
- [ ] **AC2 (re-auth gate)** вҖ” Given 'Delete account' is pressed, When the flow starts, Then a re-authentication stage renders first ('confirm it's you') and the destructive path cannot be reached by any faster route. *Assertion: `stage` state machine visits reauth before confirm; no shortcut path exists.* [Source: POC account.jsx L739-794]
- [ ] **AC3 (typed confirm)** вҖ” Given the confirm stage, When rendered, Then the destructive button stays disabled until the typed input exactly matches the account's own email OR the literal string 'DELETE'. *Assertion: `TestAccountDeleteRequiresReauthAndTypedConfirm` вҖ” button enabled iff exact match.* [Source: POC account.jsx L740-742, L796-814]
- [ ] **AC4 (business rule, honesty)** вҖ” Given confirmed deletion, When the consequence copy renders, Then it states plainly that the Library/drafts/Memory are permanently deleted but published skills others use are unclaimed (kept in the catalogue, attributed to source) вҖ” never silently deleted out from under other users. *Assertion: consequence copy names both outcomes correctly; no contradictory claim.* [Source: POC account.jsx L757-764]
- [ ] **AC5** вҖ” Given the Danger zone, When 'Delete just your Memory' is used, Then it deep-links to the Memory-scoped hard-delete flow (E3-S4) rather than duplicating that flow here вҖ” one hard-delete implementation, two entry points. *Assertion: link navigates to the existing Memory hard-delete surface; no second implementation.* [Source: POC account.jsx L765-768]

## рҹ”Ё Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestAccountDeleteRequiresReauthAndTypedConfirm` (AC2/AC3).
- [ ] GREEN: sign-out action (AC1); re-auth stage gate (AC2); typed-confirmation match logic (AC3); accurate unclaimed-not-deleted consequence copy (AC4); deep-link reuse of E3-S4's Memory hard-delete (AC5).

## рҹ§  Context

**рҹ§ҫ Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep. E3-S4 ('Sensitive-field consent UX + Enrichment Policy + hard-delete', FR-UI8) covers Memory-only hard-delete and its AC4 only requires hard-delete to be 'prominent on Memory or Account surfaces' вҖ” it has no AC for whole-account deletion, sign-out, the re-auth gate, the typed-confirm mechanic, or the unclaimed-vs-deleted business rule for published skills. Grepped feature-map.txt (740 lines) and every breakdown/*/plan.json for 'danger zone', 'delete account', 'sign out' вҖ” zero hits in the entire program.

React-front settings surface (CS В§6). Seam: Clerk re-authentication (external, true-external per CS В§5) + BFF account-deletion + skill-unclaim mutation. Sibling dependency: E3-S4 (reuses its Memory hard-delete flow via deep-link, does not duplicate it), identity's Clerk session/re-auth primitives.

## рҹ§Ә Testing

`TestAccountDeleteRequiresReauthAndTypedConfirm` (integration) + unit tests on the typed-match validator. CS В§5 mocking: Clerk re-auth (true-external mock); BFF account-deletion fixtures; never mock own stage-gate logic.

## рҹ“Ҹ Guidance

- CS `6aMAzsYeQb`: В§6 shallow view В· В§11 honest (accurate unclaimed-vs-deleted consequence copy) В· В§3 naming.
- SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: irreversible-action lens (re-auth + typed-confirm gate is the required pattern for any destructive action); no-collateral-damage lens (other users' skill usage must not break).
- Cell-lint `JGAUQRsw2g`: N/A runtime.

## рҹ”— References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (FR-UI8 family, extended to whole-account scope) В· POC `web/src/prototype/screens/account.jsx` L737-818 (ground truth) В· secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md В§3.7 (referenced in POC header comment).

## рҹ”— Dependencies

- [ ] **Blocked by:** ENG-2109 (Definition Pack вҖ” the contract TAG is the dispatch gate); E3-S4 (Memory hard-delete, reused via deep-link).
- [ ] **Parent epic:** E7 (ui: Your Wiki, Connections & Billing) вҖ” Account-settings family.
- [ ] **Intra-service order:** after E3-S4.

## рҹ“Ў Protocol

CLAIM вҶ’ PLAN вҶ’ PROGRESS вҶ’ COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) вҶ’ HANDOFF вҶ’ REVIEW (reviewer вү  implementer) вҶ’ TICK вҶ’ DONE (orchestrator-only) вҶ’ ESCALATE.
